title: Tetraria hornei
intro: Tetraria hornei, also known as Horne's club rush, is a plant species in the sedge family that is native to the eastern and southern parts of Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tetraria hornei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tetraria hornei is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Tetraria hornei is classified as a species of sedge in the family Cyperaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. It belongs to the genus Tetraria, which is a group of sedges that are native to southern Africa. The genus Tetraria is further classified within the tribe Cypereae, which is a group of sedges that are characterized by their flat, grass-like leaves. Tetraria hornei is specifically classified as a perennial sedge, meaning that it lives for more than two years and grows from underground stems. This classification helps to distinguish Tetraria hornei from other similar species of sedges.
